expectations.text: Response creates collection with Zod schema
expectations.evidence: Code defines taskSchema using z.object({...}) with fields id, title, description, status (z.enum), priority (z.number().int().min(1).max(5)), assigneeId, projectId, createdAt and uses it in createCollection

expectations.text: Response uses createCollection with getKey function
expectations.evidence: Code uses: createCollection({ id: 'tasks', schema: taskSchema, getKey: (task) => task.id, ... })

expectations.text: Response implements useLiveQuery for reactive data
expectations.evidence: TaskBoard component uses three separate useLiveQuery calls: const { data: todoTasks } = useLiveQuery((q) => q.from({ task: taskCollection }).where(...).orderBy(...))

expectations.text: Response groups tasks by status in the TaskBoard component
expectations.evidence: TaskBoard uses three live queries filtered by status ('todo', 'in-progress', 'done') and renders each group in a separate column via STATUS_COLUMNS map

expectations.text: Response includes mutation handlers (onInsert, onUpdate, onDelete)
expectations.evidence: Collection definition includes onInsert (POST to /api/tasks), onUpdate (PUT to /api/tasks/:id), and onDelete (DELETE to /api/tasks/:id) with async fetch calls
